zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle於大約1488年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾阿爾文納可時，他的父親John Killigrew39歲，母親Jane Petit37歲。他與Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1567年11月26日在他的家鄉享年80歲，埋葬在英國可瓦爾布度可 沃特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle于大约1488年出生在英国英格兰可瓦爾阿爾文納可时，他的父亲John Killigrew39岁，母亲Jane Petit37岁。他与Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard在英国结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1567年11月26日在他的家乡享年80岁，埋葬在英国可瓦爾布度可 沃特。

nameMeanings.name: Killigrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Cornish: locative name from Killigrew in Saint Erme (Cornwall).

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: First
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Fürst (see Furst ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): artificial name from Yiddish first ‘prince’.
Slovenian and Croatian (Firšt): nickname from firšt ‘prince’, an archaic or dialect loanword from German Fürst (see Furst and above).

variants.name: Furst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eugen, Kurt, Gunter, Hans, Herta, Maximilian, Ulrich.

German and Swiss German (Fürst): nickname from Old High German furisto ‘the first, most noble one’, a cognate of Old English fyrest ‘first, foremost’. The surname Fürst is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia. Compare Feerst , Fierce , Fierst , First , and Fuerst .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Fürst): artificial name from German Fürst ‘prince’. Compare Fuerst .

nameMeanings.name: Gov.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 苟, meaning ‘careless’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of the legendary Huang Di, or ‘the Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Gou (苟), the name of a fief granted to an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese surname Xun (荀) by an official in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ) in order to escape from persecution. (iv) adopted in place of the Chinese surname Gou (句) during the reign of Emperor Gaozong of Song (1107–1187 AD ), since the surname happened to share similar pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor, which was taboo in ancient China. (v) adopted in place of the Chinese surname Jing (敬) during the reign of Emperor Gaozu of Later Jin (892–942 AD ), since the surname happened to be part of the personal name of the emperor (whose personal name was Jingtang, 敬塘), which was taboo in ancient China. (vi) adopted as the surname by the Ruo Gan (若干) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勾 (or written as 句), said to be traced back to the post-name Gou Mang (勾芒 or 句芒), an official title of Chong, son of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 緱: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Zong Jing, a grandson of King Ling of Zhou (died 545  BC ). (ii) possibly from the placename Gou (緱), the name of a fief (located in Henan province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). (iii) adopted by the Ke Hou (渴侯) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Guo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Ying, Wei, Hong, Jian, Ping, Yan, Jin, Li, Xin, Min, Yi, Hua, Wen, Xuan, Quan, Que, Anh, Bang, Chang, Dai, Du, Lan, Long, Tuan, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 郭, meaning ‘rampart’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Guo (虢, also written as 郭 in ancient Chinese), the name of two ancient states during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). One was called Eastern Guo (东郭) (located in Henan province). It was granted to Guo Zhong, brother of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ), and annexed by the state of Zheng in 765  BC . The other was called Western Guo (西郭) (originally located in Shaanxi province) and was granted to Guo Shu, another brother of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou. During the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ), the Western Guo was moved to present-day Henan and Shanxi provinces, and eventually annexed by the state of Jin in 655  BC . The original place of the Western Guo was also annexed by the state of Qin in 687  BC . After the annihilation of these states, Guo (郭) was adopted as a surname. (ii) from Guo (郭), the name of another ancient state (located in Liaocheng in Shandong province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Cao during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by residents who lived outside a city, because Guo (郭) means ‘rampart, outer wall of a city’ in ancient Chinese.

variants.name: Kou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Chen-Chen, Chi, Hui, Kou, Kuei, Mei Juan, Qing, Yang.  Southeast Asian Koua, Hop, Samnang, Sau, Yen.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 寇, meaning ‘invader’ in Chinese: (i) from the second element of the post name Si Kou (司寇), the title of an official in charge of penalties during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), and in the state of Wey during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) adopted as a surname by the Gu Kou Yin (古口引) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 苟, 勾 and 緱, see   Gou   1-3.

variants.name: Hao
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Hong, Ying, Chen, Chi, Chien, Jing, Li, Lian, Ming, Sheng, Wenjie.  Korean Chang, Hu, Min, Tam.  Vietnamese Dinh, Ha, Le Van, Quan, Thi, Yen.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 郝: (i) from the placename Hao (郝), the name of a fief (located in Taiyuan in Shanxi province) granted to Zi Qi, a person during the reign of Di Yi (or Emperor Yi, died 1076  BC ), one of the rulers of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) traced back to the Wu Huan ethnic group in ancient northern China. (iii) traced back to a minority ethnic group in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Castle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Cassell
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name of Norman origin, from Cassel in the Nord department, Flanders, France.
English: sometimes a variant of   Castle  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Caisile, which is probably in origin a variant of Ó Caiside, see Cassidy .

variants.name: Castel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Herve, Jean Michel, Thierry, Yves.  Spanish Jose, Blanco, Carlos, Rafael.
English: variant of   Castle  .
Aragonese, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Castell   3, a cognate of 5 below.

variants.name: Castles
meanings: English: variant of Castle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Armagh and Meath): in Ireland, this may be the English name, but it may also be an Anglicized form of Ó Caisil; see Cashell .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle was born about 1488, in Arwennack, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Killigrew, was 39 and his mother, Jane Petit, was 37. He married Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 26 November 1567, in his hometown, at the age of 80, and was buried in Budock Water, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle est né environ 1488, à Arwennack, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Killigrew, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Jane Petit, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 26 novembre 1567, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Budock Water, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle um 1488 in Arwennack, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Killigrew 39 und seine Mutter Jane Petit 37. Er hat in England Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 26. November 1567 im Alter von 80 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Budock Water, Cornwall, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle è nato il circa 1488 nel Arwennack, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Killigrew, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Jane Petit, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 26 novembre 1567, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Budock Water, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castleが約1488年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルアルウエナックで生まれた時，父John Killigrewは39歳，母Jane Petitは37歳であった。イギリスでLady Elizabeth Trewinnardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1567年11月26日に故郷で80歳で死去し，イギリスコルンワルブドック ワターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle(이)가 대략 1488년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 아웬악크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Killigrew(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Petit(은)는 37세였다. 그는 영국에서 Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1567년 11월 26일 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 코른월 버독 왓어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle nasceu aproximadamente 1488, em Arwennack, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Killigrew, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Jane Petit, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de novembro de 1567, em sua cidade natal, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Budock Water, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle родился около 1488 г. в местности арвеннакк, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Killigrew было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Petit, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 26 ноября 1567 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности будокк вэитир, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle nació aproximadamente 1488, en Arwennack, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Killigrew, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Jane Petit, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 26 de noviembre de 1567, en su pueblo, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en Budock Water, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
